creator: johnny@johnny.kiev.ua
text:
I also have the same problem and wish to provide any help (testing, 
additional info, whatever)
I should mention, that I tried to boot from FreeBSD-6 LiveCD and 
everything works perfect, but FreeBSD-7 and CURRENT seems to be broken.

There're errors while copying files from HDD to flash and vice-versa. 
There's no difference in mount options, I tried sync, noasync, etc. 
Filesystem is msdosfs. Also I tried to reformat, recreate FS - no luck.
Fsck-ing shows errors, but correcting does nothing
** Phase 1 - Read and Compare FATs
FAT starts with odd byte sequence (0138000002380000)
Correct? no
Cluster 2 continues with cluster 14339 in FAT 0, but is marked as EOF in 
FAT 1
[skipped]

Also have a look at fdisk printing:
#fdisk /dev/da0
******* Working on device /dev/da0 *******
parameters extracted from in-core disklabel are:
cylinders=249 heads=255 sectors/track=63 (16065 blks/cyl)

parameters to be used for BIOS calculations are:
cylinders=249 heads=255 sectors/track=63 (16065 blks/cyl)

Media sector size is 512
Warning: BIOS sector numbering starts with sector 1
Information from DOS bootblock is:
The data for partition 1 is:
sysid 11 (0x0b),(DOS or Windows 95 with 32 bit FAT)
    start 32, size 4005854 (1955 Meg), flag 0
        beg: cyl 0/ head 1/ sector 1;
        end: cyl 195/ head 32/ sector 63
The data for partition 2 is:
<UNUSED>
The data for partition 3 is:
<UNUSED>
The data for partition 4 is:
<UNUSED>
